name: ServiceNow, Inc.

profile.description: ServiceNow, Inc. provides cloud-based solution for digital workflows in the North America, Europe, the Middle East and Africa, Asia Pacific, and internationally. The company provides asset management, integrated risk management, IT service management, Operational Technology management, Security Operations, strategic portfolio management, IT operations management products; customer service management product; field service management applications; and sales and order management services. It also offers human resources delivery; legal and contract operations; workplace service delivery products; app engine product; automation engine; platform privacy and security product; and source-to-pay operations. In addition, the company provides RaptorDB, a database built to manage workloads at scale; ServiceNow Impact that provides customers with software tools, guided plans, and AI-driven recommendations; customer support; and workflow data fabric. It serves government, financial services, healthcare and life science, manufacturing, Public Sector, retail, technology, and Telecom sectors through service providers and resale partners. The company has a strategic collaboration with Cohesity, Inc. to develop, operate, and safeguard autonomous AI agents and data with enterprise-grade reliability. The company was formerly known as Service-now.com and changed its name to ServiceNow, Inc. in May 2012. ServiceNow, Inc. was founded in 2004 and is headquartered in Santa Clara, California.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (2/3): ServiceNow has transformed from IT service management to an AI-powered enterprise workflow platform, with Now Assist reaching $600M ACV tracking toward $1B [1]. AI Deployment (2/2): Now Assist AI deployed at scale — customers spending $1M+ in ACV grew 130% YoY, demonstrating commercial AI traction [1]. Market Share (2/2): Dominant enterprise workflow platform with subscription revenue of $3.67B (+22% YoY). Pipeline (1/2): FY2026 guidance raised to $15.7-15.8B subscription revenue. Armis acquisition expanding into security. R&D Intensity (1/1): Heavy R&D in AI and platform capabilities.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): Making work better for people — genuine and reflected in platform design. Community Impact (1/2): Technology access programs. Employee Retention (1/2): Strong tech culture under CEO McDermott. Insider Buying (2/2): McDermott's leadership and equity alignment demonstrate conviction.

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored. Q1 2026 subscription revenue of $3.67B grew 22% YoY [1]. Guidance raised to $15.7-15.8B for full year. Now Assist tracking from $600M toward $1B ACV. Strong recurring revenue model with high retention rates. The $7.75B Armis acquisition expands the security TAM.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (6/9): ServiceNow's workflow platform has deep enterprise integrations and high switching costs that protect against AI disruption [1]. However, AI-native workflow tools could theoretically bypass ServiceNow's platform. The company is well-positioned to be an AI enabler rather than victim. AI Opportunity (1/1): Now Assist at $600M ACV growing 130%+ demonstrates significant AI revenue.

infinite_game.summary: ServiceNow under CEO Bill McDermott demonstrates infinite game thinking — the platform expansion from ITSM to enterprise-wide AI workflows reflects a long-term vision of making work better. McDermott's track record (SAP, ServiceNow) suggests genuine commitment to building enduring enterprises.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): Making the world of work better is genuine and reflected in platform decisions [1].

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): Competes constructively with Salesforce and Microsoft.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): Evolved from ITSM to enterprise AI platform.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (2/2): $7.75B Armis acquisition and massive AI investment demonstrate willingness to make transformative bets [1].

investment_thesis: ServiceNow delivered Q1 2026 subscription revenue of $3.67B (+22%) with Now Assist AI reaching $600M ACV and tracking toward $1B [1]. Customers spending $1M+ on AI grew 130% YoY. The $7.75B Armis acquisition expands into security. Guidance raised to $15.7-15.8B subscription revenue. ServiceNow is emerging as the enterprise AI workflow platform of choice.
thesis_continued: Premium valuation (high forward P/E) requires sustained 20%+ growth — any deceleration would compress multiples significantly [1]. The $7.75B Armis acquisition carries integration risk and EPS dilution. Microsoft Copilot integration into the Office suite could compete for enterprise AI workflow budgets. Customer spending consolidation during economic uncertainty could slow deal velocity.
key_catalysts: Now Assist scaling from $600M toward $1B ACV as enterprise AI adoption accelerates [1]
Armis integration expanding the security and compliance TAM for ServiceNow's platform
FY2026 subscription revenue guidance raised to $15.7-15.8B, implying continued 20%+ growth
key_risks: Microsoft Copilot + Power Platform competing for enterprise AI workflow budgets
Premium valuation requiring sustained 20%+ growth with no room for execution misses
Armis integration complexity and $7.75B acquisition potentially diluting returns
sources: Yahoo Finance — NOW Key Statistics, accessed June 2026
